questions.question_text: You are approaching a signal-controlled intersection and want to continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have driven too far and want to turn as soon as possible. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have caught up with the two slowly moving cars with caravans. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You have reduced speed in order to turn left at the intersection. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 70 km/h. What do you do?
What is the highest sensible speed through this bend?
You are driving at about 30 to 40 km/h. What do you do?
You have moved to the middle of the road to turn left. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
Which speed rules should you pay particular attention to here?
You are driving on a one-way road and want to continue straight past the roadworks. What should you pay particular attention to?
You have caught up with the slowly moving driver ahead. What do you do?
What does the police officer's hand signal mean?
You want to continue straight ahead. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are driving at about 30 km/h, the same speed as the car ahead. What do you do?
You are about to catch up with the slowly moving car ahead at about 40 km/h. There is no traffic behind you. What do you do?
You are in a hurry and have caught up with the car ahead. It is travelling at about 30 km/h. What do you do?
You must continue straight ahead at the intersection. There is nothing behind you and the motorcyclist ahead is slowing down. What do you do?
You want to turn right as soon as possible. What do you do here?
You want to turn right at this junction. What do you do?
You are driving at about 40 km/h and intend to continue straight ahead through this junction. What do you do?
You have reduced speed in order to turn left at the intersection. There are no cars or motorcycles behind you. What do you do?
You are about to enter a motorway, but the driver ahead is hesitating about merging. There is no traffic behind you in the nearest motorway lane. What do you do?
You are driving at about 60 km/h and have caught up with the car on a long, steep uphill section. What do you do?
You have just got out of the car. Which rules apply here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
answers.2: I may leave the car here for loading or unloading.

questions.question_text: You have got out of the red car. Which rule applies here?

answers.1: I may leave the car here for 2-3 minutes.
